programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An environmental campaigner is suspected of starting a Bush fire to prevent developers building a new estate in the tranquil Malibu Hills - until he becomes a victim of the flames himself

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An heiress is stabbed by a stalker, but the most likely suspect is found dead - and the case is complicated when an X-ray of the victim reveals surprising results. Medical whodunit, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: In a corrupt town, Richard Kimble is savagely beaten by two corrupt policemen who are under orders of the one-armed man

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry takes on an intriguing case at the San Diego Zoo involving a kidnapped baby gorilla and a dentist found mauled to death in the lion enclosure

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker faces a man who seems to be the reincarnation of a Native American witch doctor, and time-travels to the 19th century to right past wrongs and stop history repeating itself

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio grows convinced the murder of a police officer in New York is linked to the killing of a Miami teenager's parents - but cannot see how the suspect could have travelled between the cities so quickly. Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes (CSI: NY) guest star alongside regulars David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself in a tight spot when he is mistakenly identified as the murderer of a neighbourhood girl while working as a janitor

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A museum worker is released from prison after being falsely accused of vandalism, only to find himself charged with murdering the man who framed him

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A dramatic undercover operation results in the death of a serial killer, seemingly bringing the case to an abrupt conclusion. However, it gradually becomes clear that he may have worked with an accomplice - and evidence emerges to implicate the new man in Ziva's life. Drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Trivette is the intended victim of a vengeance killing after defending the daughter of a rodeo owner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: NCIS

Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey discovers more than she bargained for while sorting out jumble for a car-boot sale after chancing upon an old photo album. Linda Robson stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gina reports Honey as a missing person when she fails to report for work and it's not long before her fingerprints are found at Bonham Wharf, identifying her as the second prisoner at the scene. Zain panics as the net closes on him, but Kristen remains calm and reassures him they can make a clean getaway. John Heaton's wife tells him they are through and Emma finds out just how controlling her new husband can be

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael receives a shock when a nurse he once had an affair with begins working on his private ward. Meanwhile, Linden is forced to put his own feelings to one side as he helps Faye with the emotional fallout of her break-up with Joseph, and Elliot's prodigal daughter Martha returns from Las Vegas with some surprising news. Hari Dhillon, Paul Bradley and Duncan Pow star

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Simon reacts badly to Lara's news that Harry wants to replace him temporarily, but he's soon cheered up when he feels a slight sensation in his legs. Fin is thwarted in his bid to patch things up with Comfort after she brings Luke home and gets drunk. Nikki discovers she is pregnant and Charlie is determined to fight for Louis

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Sun Hill watch are briefed about Honey's death and Will is embarrassed to learn her mother wants to meet him as she has heard so much about him. Zain comes under intense questioning from the Murder Investigation Team but manages to give Phil and Neil the slip in order to meet up with Kristen and make his escape

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle's thrown by Geoff's mention of a possible job in America. David and Cindy are nearly caught by Ian in the portacabin. David convinces Ian they can win the pub quiz by cheating

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kath misses Phil and is nervous about the baby. David and Ian's pub quiz scam fails. Ruth worries that Grant heard her tell the paramedics that Mark is HIV positive

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah returns to Inverness with Rene, but may have underestimated her feelings for George. Olivia finds the burden of deception weighs heavily, while Anna and Gino prepare for their wedding day

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will's sympathy for a couple of old friends contemplating fertility treatment soon turns to irritation when they opt to take matters into their own hands. Sam blows some serious cash on Kerri _ who, along with Tom, is in line for yet another surprise. Rural medical drama, starring Joseph Millson, Susannah Corbett and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James must break the sad news that a dog owner's beloved pet is blind, and Tristan is given an ultimatum by Siegfried. Christopher Timothy, Peter Davison and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey resolves to sort out his marital difficulties by agreeing to a divorce. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin commissions a statue of himself and issues strict instructions for the unveiling - paving the way for more chaos and confusion at the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly jumps to the conclusion that Barry is up to no good after he is spotted acting suspiciously, but his real motive for being in the woods with a suitcase is unfathomable. Long-running comedy, staring Mike Grady, Frank Thornton and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a body is found half-buried in a swamp. Visiting her home, Wallander discovers signs of an intruder and that the dead woman's daughter is missing. The return of a lost love distracts him from the case at hand, as he encounters a gang of bikers that have been causing trouble in the area and begins to suspect he is being watched

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Ros's membership of Yalta is revealed after the organisation informs her of its plan to overthrow the American government. Working for both sides becomes her only option if she is to nullify the threat and save her own life, but as Harry himself leads the team into the traitors' headquarters hoping to unmask the ringleaders, it might all be too late to stop the operation codenamed Floodland

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Sun Hill watch are briefed about Honey's death and Will is embarrassed to learn her mother wants to meet him as she has heard so much about him. Zain comes under intense questioning from the Murder Investigation Team but manages to give Phil and Neil the slip in order to meet up with Kristen and make his escape

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part special. The women of Cranford are excited about the imminent visit of Lady Glenmire, until the Hon Mrs Jamieson announces that none of them is of high enough social standing to meet her - so Miss Matty leads the Amazons in snubbing both the newcomer and her hostess. But sociable Lady Glenmire is quick to offer an olive branch by hosting a memorable Halloween party, which results in the forging of new friendships. Mr Buxton is impressed by Edward Bell's ambitions, so takes him on as land agent, only for the new employee to make a disastrous decision that has dire consequences for both himself and his sister Peggy. Meanwhile, Matty is distressed when a surprising discovery about one of her close circle results in a serious falling out among the friends, and when tragedy strikes the town, she fears Cranford may never recover. Judi Dench, Celia Imrie and Jonathan Pryce star

Meanwhile, Tom tells Alex it would be better for both of them if they stayed just good friends. Maggie O'Neill, Joseph Millson and Gray O'Brien star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James, Siegfried and Tristan find different ways of dealing with their domestic problems, and Mrs Hall makes a surprise announcement. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy's penfriend comes to stay, but some of the staff are not convinced his intentions are entirely honourable. Comedy, starring Su Pollard

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A wave of graffiti becomes a problem in Maplin's, so the staff goes in search of the culprit. Barry injures his back while trying to clean up, leading Yvonne to call on an old dancing partner to help out, while Fred learns new things about himself. Starring Simon Cadell and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: While Richard contends with the idea of taking early retirement, Hyacinth telephones the prime minister asking if there is anything on the honours list for him. programme.desc: Frankie concocts a chilling ruse to expose a conman who claims he can speak to the dead. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a political activist 15 years earlier but, just as it is getting somewhere, Ted orders the investigation to be halted and Ucos is threatened with closure following its perceived mishandling of the Hanway case. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team is suspended following Ros's betrayal, but that leaves the agents in the line of fire for a disgruntled former IRA terrorist about to detonate a bomb in the heart of London. Adam decides the only way to prevent America going to war with Iran is to reveal the truth about the Tehran train explosion, but doing so would destroy all hopes of finding a diplomatic solution

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Investigating the mysterious death of a youngster at an elite Glasgow school, Burke incurs the wrath of the local press and finds himself branded a bully for his rough tactics. Ross goes undercover and soon discovers links with the shady world of drug-dealing. Drama, starring Blythe Duff and Alex Norton

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey find their glamorous new jobs in a casino have a hidden downside, while Dorien rekindles an old flame

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: As the crucial final match approaches, Fisher asks Crabbe to keep an eye on the police football ground and look after an ex-professional player with a murky past - but there may be more to his new protege than meets the eye. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A teenage apprentice starts work in the restaurant kitchens. At first, he seems a decent, hard-working lad, but then disappears - along with the takings from a busy evening. Richard Griffiths and Kevin Bishop star

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sean Bean returns as Bernard Cornwell's swashbuckling hero, this time setting out to rescue British deserters captured by Spanish rebel El Casco. Along the way he crosses swords with a fellow officer and come to the aid of a damsel in distress. Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and John Tams also star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: After a 10-year-old girl is injured in a petrol bomb attack by animal rights activists, an outraged Gene takes Alex to interview a veteran campaigner in jail. Though the prisoner is weak and incoherent after going on hunger strike, Alex is convinced he not only has details of planned attacks - he may also know about her own situation and be able to help her get home. Guest starring David Bradley and Rory Kinnear

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene receives a visit from Jackie Queen, a journalist from Manchester, who announces to the shocked superintendent she is pregnant with his baby. She is also investigating the disappearance of teenagers suspected of running away to London, so the team launches an investigation and a suspect is quickly identified. However, it becomes clear the man is being protected by someone within the Met. John Bowe and Ruth Millar guest star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A schoolgirl's killing brings Gently into the alien world of pop and media celebrity when it turns out the victim's best friend is a rising TV star. Bacchus suspects the dead girl's music teacher, since rumours persist that she was having an affair with him. But when it seems everyone has a different opinion of the girl, Gently must uncover these different faces to get to the truth of her murder. Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A couple stumble upon the body of an elderly man in the old hospital wing of an abandoned mental health facility, strapped to a gurney with electrodes connected to his temples

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The Fisher household acquires a new wireless receiver, while Dot realises that she doesn't want to relinquish working for Phryne when she marries. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Ashleigh Cummings

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective encounters escape artist Harry Houdini when he finds himself investigating a daring bank robbery involving the murder of one of its guards, while Julia's sister Ruby pays Toronto a visit and soon becomes attracted to the sleuth. Yannick Bisson stars

The volunteers plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.desc: A woman claiming she tripped on a loose paving stone as she pushed her toddler in a buggy, is caught out when CCTV footage reveals the truth. Plus, information embedded in a digital photo scuppers an attempted scam for lost jewellery. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A skier suffers a leg injury in a remote part of the Alps, so her guide tries to carry her to safety on his back. A girl calls 999 when her mum goes into anaphylactic shock

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion digs up some of the best kitchen and garden antiques that the red and blue teams have purchased around the country over the years. She also shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring antique fairs and centres. Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a unique collection of teapots in Kent, while Eric Knowles delves into the history and collectability of bone china

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protein shake made from dried and powdered crickets, washable cleaning cloths, and an app for locating children that have wandered off

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with two sets of buyers whose rural aspirations were driven by a spirit of adventure. She first heads to the Welsh Borders for a reunion with a couple who managed to buy their first home - a rural farmhouse with space to accommodate their many hobbies and dogs - in 2017. Nicki then moves on to Devon's south coast to revisit a couple of surfing enthusiasts who, after reassessing their priorities, have successfully built a new life by the sea with their young son

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore uses her salvaging expertise at Witley Recycling Centre to search for three items that she can clean up and reinvent. To help her, she calls on trusty makers Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Duncan McKean. Leigh-Anne takes on two wingback armchairs in need of a makeover, while lighting expert Duncan has some retro lampshades to contend with. Sarah herself sees potential in an extendable dining table

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp hunt for antiques in the Derbyshire Dales. They both shop until they drop, buying everything from delicate glassware to hearty rustic pieces, with an animal-shaped cocktail shaker thrown into the mix. Along the way, Margie explores a world of splendour in an historic 550-acre garden, tucked away in a very surprising location. Natasha discovers a renowned artist whose intricate and breath-taking paintings and drawings illuminated the spirit of the age in which he lived

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon warns Linda about her suspicions about Max, but she dismisses it, claiming he is just a friend. But when he turns up later, Linda becomes guarded, wondering if her friend has a point. Ian arranges an interview with the Walford Gazette to talk about his business empire - unaware of what is being revealed behind his back

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games. Host Richard has some surprises up his sleeve, including a House of Games House Band, a sneaky peak at some other rooms in his House of Games and a few surprise, special guests. A weekly winner will be declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show as the scores are tallied across the five-part series, resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz, inviting writer Joan Bakewelll and comedian Fin Taylor to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: It's Sunday in the house of the couch-potato clan and Nana calls round. Denise tries on her wedding dress, but nothing can stand in the way of Antiques Roadshow. Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, following the lives of a working-class Manchester family and set almost exclusively in their living room. With Ricky Tomlinson, Sue Johnston and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Romesh Ranganathan promotes his new book As Good As It Gets: Life Lessons from a Reluctant Adult and Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens embark on their first Drag Race UK tour. Hosted by Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards, the event sees The Vivienne, Baga Chipz, Divina de Campo, Cheryl Hole, Blu Hydranga, Sum Ting Wong, Vinegar Strokes, Scaredy Kat, and Gothy Kendoll perform in venues in six UK cities. Alyssa - who promises to bring a seasoned and steady hand to the stage - arrives to find the gaggle of UK queens scrambling to get their collective act together. The problem is that curtain-up is only hours away

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: After only three days of rehearsals, the UK queens - along with Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards - raise the curtain on the sold-out opening night of the first-ever UK Drag Race tour. However, a microphone malfunction threatens to silence the show before it even starts

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ollie Dabbous and Asma Khan, and special guest Omid Djalili. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Liverpool v Leicester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about Derby's connection to the hot dog

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. There was an upset in last year's corresponding contest when Stefanos Tsitsipas overcame six-time champion Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite the stars of sci-fi and fantasy to take part in a special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Newcastle and Chelsea were both looking to emulate impressive victories in last season's corresponding encounters

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v Arizona Cardinals

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah revisits two sets of Escape to the Country house-hunters united by their appreciation of traditional, period homes. In Wiltshire, she catches up with a couple for whom it was love at first sight with picture-perfect character cottages shown to them on their 2019 house hunt. Heading to Hampshire, Sonali meets another buyer who succeeded in finding her dream home - a property filled with positive energy where she could practise meditation in a serene setting

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a broken-up sideboard, two rattan chairs and wooden bed ends from the recycling centre, hoping she and her expert friends can turn them into money-making items

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: The life of 57-year-old Lorraine, a tribute act to the American pop star Pink, exploring what it takes to spend your life pretending to be someone else

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following residents of a pioneering Welsh eco-village Brithdir Mawr, who are attempting to raise £1million to save their homes and their way of life before the lease expires. The documentary follows the community over the course of a year as they try to save their homes and preserve the eco-hamlet for posterity

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Katie tackle the difficult issue of taking their burgeoning relationship to the next level, while Arlo is still struggling to work things out with Lindsey and Poppy. Elsewhere, Shay is led astray by Siouxsie, and Big Mac launches a plan to entice more tourists to Weld. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey through dishes prepared by an array of TV chefs, beginning with recipes and ingredients linked by the letter A. Highlights include Jamie Oliver's savoury apple crumble and Sophie Dahl's toffee apple crumble

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day five from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Italy's Matteo Berrettini won 7-6, 6-3 against Austria's Dominic Thiem in this session last year, but it was Thiem who went on to progress to the final

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals why male seahorses give birth and looks at how hyena packs are dominated by the female members. He also examines creatures that spin and weave, looking into the processes involved in the complex structures of webs and nests

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Nick Hall assess items brought to Boconnoc House in Cornwall, and presenter Paul Martin participates in a traditional Cornish gig race

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Shirley Ballas talks about the show and answers viewers' questions and costume designer Vicky Gill shows what some of the couples will be wearing this weekend

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working in the gas industry in and around the Cumbrian coast. She travels by helicopter to the Morecambe gas field as engineers find new ways of extracting the remaining fuel supply that has been exploited for more than 35 years. Keeley also heads for the Barrow gas terminal where a complex network of pipes sends the gas to local homes

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom visits the Black Bull in the village of Gartmore in Stirling. Struggling to survive, it was put on the market and 250 locals clubbed together to buy it. Now the pub is staffed entirely by volunteers and the management are planning to renovate the bedrooms to attract tourists. But Tom has his concerns. He also catches up with the staff of the pubs he is helping in Cornwall, south London and Stroud - but just when all the hard work seems to be paying off, the pandemic strikes. Now every pub in Britain faces a fight for survival

programme.title: Lockdown 1.0 - Following the Science?
programme.desc: An investigation into just how closely Boris Johnson and the Government followed the scientific evidence throughout the early months of the coronavirus pandemic. Scientists at the heart of Britain's response to the virus reveal what happened behind the scenes, and how problems accessing key data caused flaws in the modelling process that meant fighting the virus was an uphill battle from the very beginning

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Zoe Lyons and Michael Odewale. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in May 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the second episode, Angela Scanlon, Amol Rajan and Emma Barnett take a light-hearted look back at the year 2001, from the first Harry Potter film to hit cinemas, to a gripping EastEnders storyline

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: In a Halloween-themed programme, Gillian Burke shows off the amazing hydraulic power of spiders' legs. Iolo Williams has an exclusive update on the remarkable story of a giant vulture that set up home in Derbyshire, Megan McCubbin and Chris Packham look back at the mouse experiment and their friendly foxes, and Michaela Strachan catches up on the highs and lows of the Scottish seal colony

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Norfolk, where he catches up with a couple who have developed a new business and a duo now enjoying the simple life in the East Anglian countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving two metal chairs to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a loom chair and cabinet to furniture restyler Chloe Kempster. For his own project, Jay transforms a pair of old wardrobe doors

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George agrees to go camping with Katie, but their ideas of what that entails are very different and they end up being followed on the trip by half the people they know

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a journey of culinary discovery, taking confectionery lessons around the country before making a five-tier wedding cake. They learn about the art of chocolate in Otley, West Yorkshire, and meet a Manchester couple willing to let the pair loose on their big day

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day six from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Rafael Nadal won 6-7, 6-4, 7-5 against Stefanos Tsitsipas at this stage last year, but it was Tsitsipas who progressed from the group and went on to win the title

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough explores animal oddities, looking at two different creatures with curiously distinctive evolutionary quirks, linked together by a common theme. He examines how hedgehogs and rhinos have developed protective skins, and looks at animals that rely on other species, from cuckoos planting their eggs in other birds' nests to the Death's-head hawkmoth stealing honey

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, antiques expert Catherine Southon enthuses about a diamond ring and Charles Hanson enjoys trying on a pair of boxing gloves signed by some of the greats of the boxing world. Presenter Paul Martin discovers the skeleton of a 58-foot sperm whale, which was washed up on a nearby beach in Victorian times

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: A trio of celebrity superfans discuss all things ballroom amid analysis of the couples' progress. Gethin Jones is at Elstree Studios with the latest news from behind the scenes

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark hosts this fundraising special in which Dara O Briain and Fay Ripley lead teams from the BBC and ITV. Competing with them are Anita Rani, Steve Pemberton, Iain Stirling, Joel Dommett, Charlene White and Dane Baptiste, but who has the knowledge to make it to the top of the class?

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actor and comedian Ben Miller, broadcaster Grace Dent, comedian Desiree Burch and presenter Graham Norton to share their favourite books, reveal the books they pretend to have read but haven't and the book they would give someone on a first date. The panellists also discuss this week's book group pick, The Devil and the Dark Water by Stuart Turton, as well as Graham Norton's latest novel, Home Stretch

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan and Dr Lukarevsky keep a close eye on Koshka's family as they continue on the road to full recovery. They have to prove their ability to hunt and survive before they can be released back to the wild. Bryansk has become so domesticated it's a major challenge for Gordon and Victor to help him find his wild instincts. If he cannot he will not be able to be released and will have to spend the rest of his life in captivity

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In October 1917, the Bolsheviks rose up, swept the Russian tsar from power and communism was born. However, the exact facts of the Russian Revolution are disputed. Here, the historian finds it was really a group of women workers who kick-started the event nine months earlier. At the time, the Bolsheviks tried to stop it, and Lenin - radical leader of the Bolsheviks - wasn't even in the country. Lucy also discovers that the tsar was forced to abdicate long before the Bolsheviks took control and that King George V betrayed his cousin by opposing the British government's offer of asylum to the tsar

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: 1985 sees the 'Big Four' of 80's Pop - Spandau, Wham, Culture Club and the Durans - have a relatively quiet year which ushers in a new generation of pop talent. The show becomes a barometer for the state of the nation as the ambitious King and A-ha, propelled by the most expensive video of the year for Take On Me, compete for attention with passionate politicos Billy Bragg and The Style Council who, fired up by the ongoing miners' strike and continuing threat of nuclear war, give the studio audience their all with Between The Wars and Walls Come Tumbling Down. 1985 also sees electro dance hit the mainstream as Dead or Alive top the charts with You Spin Me Round, the first number one for producers Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and Paul Hardcastle holds the top slot for five weeks with 19, a spoken word single made with new sampling technology and released on the 10th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam war. Meanwhile, movie tie-ins and record companies eyeing a 'housewives' market see a

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: With the fleet now reunited, old conflicts begin to resurface, as tensions between civilians and the military reach boiling point. Colonel Tigh receives death threats following the massacre on the Gideon. President Roslin asks a reporter onboard to help improve relations, but does not count on her discovering one of Galactica's most explosive secrets. Guest starring Lucy Lawless

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Captured agent Sharon reveals to Adama that a Cylon virus has infected the Galactica's computers, scanning them for weaknesses and tampering with the ship's basic internal functions in preparation for a major attack on the vessel. The Commander must decide whether she can be trusted, while the flight crew is hard at work on a new type of fighter named 'the Blackbird'

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood discover some uninvited animal guests, including a herd of elephants in a hotel, and an ant that indulges in some shocking behaviour

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and company discover a carrot cake that does not contain any carrots in Singapore, and explore the hilltop city of Shimla, where the British Raj spent the Indian summer

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A young boy called Aron loves all things Bollywood, especially dancing, so the team builds him his very own Bollywood-style den

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Online superstar and CBBC presenter Nikki Lilly is in the studio to demonstrate some amazing life hacks from her new book, there is a look back at Lindsey's epic Children in Need challenge and the presenters attempt a dance challenge

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve looks back at the most awesome experiments over the series and chooses his highlights. He then uses them to build the ultimate deadly dino and create a crazy hybrid

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Designers Ryan White and Zoe Laughlin explore if there is anything they can do to help 11-year-old Malachi, who has Tourette's syndrome and ADHD, control his tics and behavioural issues. Plus, a challenge to get a four-year-old who is paralysed from the waist down walking again, and helping a quadriplegic man use his mobile and credit card when he is out and about without having to ask for help

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles between homemade fighting robots, including a towering, sword-wielding warrior built by a Mensa member and his three sons, that is one of the tallest machines to ever enter the arena and runs on wheels that are the height of a washing machine. Other contestants include a mechanical lobster and a 19-year-old self-taught engineer's creation, which has teeth that spin at 9000RPM. The conflict takes a dramatic turn when one of the robots bursts into flames

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Live Women's International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v France (Kick-off 12.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents all the action from the autumn international at Twickenham. The sides also met in France last weekend, but regardless of that outcome, England will be expecting to prevail on this occasion given their dominant record against their opponents on home soil. With commentary by Sara Orchard

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of David Lean, whose drive and genius made him one of Britain's greatest ever film directors. Featuring a compilation of rarely seen interviews and archive footage. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made by actor Noel Coward, featuring archive interview clips showing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1945)
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, starring Celia Johnson and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom visits the Black Bull in the Stirlingshire village of Gartmore. Struggling to survive, it was put on the market and 250 locals clubbed together to buy it. Now the pub is staffed entirely by volunteers and the management are planning to renovate the bedrooms to attract tourists. But Tom has his concerns. He also catches up with the staff of the pubs he is helping in Cornwall, south London and Stroud - but just when all the hard work seems to be paying off, the pandemic strikes. Now every pub in Britain faces a fight for survival

programme.title: The River: A Year in the Life of the Tay
programme.desc: Writer and naturalist Helen Macdonald examines life along the River Tay, following the river's course from Ben Lui in the West to Dundee in the East, encountering salmon, beavers and the microscopic creatures and learning about the history of the Britain's largest river

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of 'radio activity', with Joe Lycett, Shazia Mirza, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Fela Kuti: Father of Afrobeat
programme.desc: The story of the influential African musician, who is remembered not only as the creator of the Afrobeat genre but also as an outspoken political revolutionary who criticised military regimes in his native Nigeria. A counter-cultural revolutionary, he fought injustice with his music and provoked society with his lifestyle, on one occasion marrying 27 wives in a single ceremony. When he died in 1997 from HIV, a disease that carried huge stigma in Africa, there was fear his legacy would die with him, but this film shows that the impact of his life and music is still felt today

programme.title: Lynn + Lucy (2019)
programme.desc: Having grown up together, young mothers Lynn and Lucy are inseparable - neighbours and best friends, they are as close as can be. But when tragedy befalls the pair, a chain of events is set in motion that severely tests their bond. Mystery drama, starring Roxanne Scrimshaw, Nichola Burley and Kacey Ainsworth

programme.title: Sus (2010)
programme.desc: A black man is taken in for questioning when his wife is found murdered on the night of the 1979 general election. He comes to realise that the police have already assumed he killed her, while the racist detectives interrogating him use increasingly brutal methods to force him into a false confession. Drama, with Clint Dyer, Ralph Brown, Rafe Spall and Anjela Lauren Smith

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah demonstrates the engineering and design skills that shaped Scottish baronial style. He visits Glamis Castle to show how the sandstone tower was transformed by Italian plasterers to resemble a fairy-tale castle, and the House of Dun near Montrose, designed by William Adam

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints birch trees, surrounded by a misty background and a cheerful blue sky

programme.title: Easter Parade (1948)
programme.desc: A dancer abandoned by his usual partner trains a chorus girl as a replacement. The new pairing is a great success but, although he strives to keep their relationship strictly professional, he begins to fall head over heels in love with her. Musical, with Fred Astaire, Judy Garland, Peter Lawford and Ann Miller. Songs include A Couple of Swells, Stepping Out with My Baby and Shaking the Blues Away

programme.title: Puttin' on the Ritz: The Genius of Fred Astaire
programme.desc: Fred Astaire's definitive performance of the song and dance routine, as recorded in the late 1920s by Irving Berlin, and later featured in his 1946 musical Blue Skies

programme.title: Country Music Awards 2020
programme.desc: Highlights from the 54th Annual Country Music Association Awards, staged on Wednesday November 11 at the Music City Centre in Nashville, Tennessee. The event was hosted by CMA award-winners Darius Rucker and Reba McEntire

programme.title: Country at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC session performances by country artists from the past four decades, including Tammy Wynette, Emmylou Harris, Johnny Cash, Dolly Parton, Charley Pride, Kris Kristofferson, Garth Brooks, Willie Nelson, Alison Krauss, Taylor Swift, kd lang and Billie Jo Spears

programme.title: Country Queens at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances by female country-music stars from a variety of BBC studio shows. Featured acts include Bobbie Gentry, Anne Murray, Emmylou Harris, Taylor Swift, Crystal Gayle, Tammy Wynette, Billie Jo Spears, and Lucinda Williams with Mary Chapin Carpenter

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints birch trees, surrounded by a misty background and a cheerful blue sky

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah demonstrates the engineering and design skills that shaped Scottish baronial style. He visits Glamis Castle to show how the sandstone tower was transformed by Italian plasterers to resemble a fairy-tale castle, and the House of Dun near Montrose, designed by William Adam

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores how cathedrals were constructed, looking at fan vaulting at Gloucester, stained glass at York, and the Octagon at Ely for examples of medieval ingenuity, showing how they were built to realise a vision of Heaven on Earth

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent embarks on a joyful journey back through the BBC archives, celebrating the broadcaster's song-and-dance extravaganzas of the 1960s, 1970s and 1980s. A host of stars are featured, such as Cilla Black, Cliff Richard, Lulu, Twiggy and Shirley Bassey, as well as some of the biggest dance troupes of the time, including Pan's People and The Young Generation

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on March 29, with performances by artists including Bizz Nizz, Candy Flip, Jam Tronik, Heart, Jimmy Somerville, the Family Stand, Queen Latifah and De La Soul and Snap

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on April 5, with performances by artists including Jason Donovan, They Might Be Giants, Happy Mondays, Madonna, Queen Latifah and De La Soul, Emma, UB40, Snap and Alannah Myles

programme.title: Jazz 625: The British Jazz Explosion
programme.desc: The BBC Two programme Jazz 625 originally aired in the mid-1960s and featured some of the greatest legends of that era. Here, drummer Moses Boyd and 1Xtra presenter Jamz Supernova pay homage to that original run and also introduce contemporary performances by Sons of Kemet, Nubya Garcia, Kokoroko, Poppy Ajudha, Matthew Halsall, Moses Boyd and Exodus and Ezra Collective, all of whom may be considered stars of the current UK jazz explosion. Plus, short films exploring the roots of the current UK jazz scene

programme.title: Miles Davis: Birth of the Cool (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary profile of the jazz bandleader, horn player and innovator whose restless determination to break boundaries and live life on his own terms made him a star, but also made him difficult to live with. In music and in life, Davis shied away from the status quo, and when he thought his work came to represent a new convention, he changed it again. Featuring studio out-takes, archive footage and photos and Davis's own words, as well as contributions from many friends and colleagues

programme.title: Ronnie's: Ronnie Scott & His World-Famous Jazz Club
programme.desc: The history of Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club, eponymously named after the late tenor saxophonist who founded the establishment with business partner Pete King. It opened in London's Soho in October 1959, inspired by the vibrant post-war jazz venues in New York. Since then, Ronnie's has consistently played host to many of the world's greatest jazz legends, including Chet Baker, Count Basie, Miles Davies, Ella Fitzgerald, Buddy Rich and Nina Simone who are all featured in the film. Contributors include Ronnie's friends, family and the music elite, including Chris Blackwell, Quincy Jones and Sonny Rollins

programme.title: Country Music Awards 2020
programme.desc: Highlights from the 54th Annual Country Music Association Awards, staged on Wednesday November 11 at the Music City Centre in Nashville, Tennessee. The event was hosted by CMA award-winners Darius Rucker and Reba McEntire

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Author and architect Francesco da Mosto explores the countryside of Tuscany, Umbria and the Marche. He prepares pasta and picks grapes, before joining actress Maggie Smith to view Florence's art treasures. He also relates the story of St Francis of Assisi and visits a garden once owned by a British woman who fell in love with an Italian aristocrat

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and intrepid explorer visits Bolivia and Peru, where he meets an Englishwoman restoring a 140-year-old steamer. He then follows the 1,000-mile Urubamba River and sees the Inca ruins of Machu Picchu

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Ambitious detective Katrin 'Kata' Gunnarsdottir investigates when an ex-drug dealer is found murdered in the old harbour area of Reykjavik, and finds that she does not have as much control of her professional life as she had planned. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Detective Arnar Bodvarsson arrives from Oslo to help Kata find a link between the two murders. At the memorial to the latest victim, they catch the prime suspect, who had broken into his father's office. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: Icelandic Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a 38-mile journey along Iceland's most famous hiking route. She navigates daunting mountain climbs, red-hot lava fields, freezing river crossings and deadly clouds of sulphuric gas to reach the volcanic crater at the centre of the Eyjafjallajokull glacier

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Swedish journalist and radical left-wing expert Richard Dahlqvist broadcasts live on the internet, denying Red October's involvement in the murder. Saga and Henrik question him, finding what he is says is wide of the truth. They also continue to question Taariq, though finding him unwilling to cooperate form a plan to expose him. The duo search the homeless Danish girls' hiding place, finding evidence that directs them to new lead. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Still at large, Taariq spots the car used to kidnap Margrethe Thormod, and tracks down its owner with the intention of extorting money from him. Meanwhile the girls vacate Henrik's house, and while trying to find them Henrik runs into Thomas, the drug dealer. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

This means he has to choose a winner between his friends – Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two – the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster's small kite doesn't seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.
