programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Veteran seafarer Old Jack recalls how Sheena the Shrimp tried to keep everyone warm with the overgrown seaweed

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley goes to a farm in Northamptonshire to find out all about the world's biggest birds - ostriches. JB Gill discovers how cobnuts are grown and gathered in Kent

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having fun. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, is helping her mum cook tea along with little sister Tracy. In Canada, eight-year-old Rhys is learning how to ice skate - and taking a few tumbles along the way. And in Italy, seven-year-old Mario is escaping from the hot sun by playing a diving game in the swimming pool with his brother and sister

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ottalie's friend Noah comes to play. They spend the morning making an outside den with big sticks and blankets. Mum and little sister Imogen join them in the den for lunch. They also get creative and make a giant picture using lots of paint and sand

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory meets a farm vet who is checking a flock of lambs. Ferne visits a zoo's vet team

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day looks at baby animals tricky first steps. Polar bear cubs slip on the arctic snow. In Africa a baby elephant needs mum's help to stand up. Mandarin ducklings face a huge leap from their tree trunk nest in Russia

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Norbet is learning to play Squeezy-Box, but Deeno cannot concentrate

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is counting fish with Fisherman Tumble, while Justin and his friends are counting animals at a wildlife park

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how a tram uses electricity to work and discovers how a map is made

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug meet Sherlock Holmes and Watson when they decide to solve a mystery

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Mr Bouncer invents something that seemingly has no specific function, and the rabbits try to find out what it is for

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch turns the canals of Venice into roads

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are making Great Gran Gran's St. Lucian Callaloo soup, but they can’t buy the last few vegetables they need because Jared’s shop is closed. Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan, and with Cynthia’s help JoJo harvests her own vegetables from the allotment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie forgets her reading book, so Waffle takes it to her PE class at school

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits Katy at her croft. They see her favourite cows - Isla and Riona the calf - then give them food and fresh water and make Riona a cow cake. Later, the vet comes to check how Riona is doing

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with bath toys and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: The team helps turn a tiny courtyard into a huge playground

programme.title: Puss In Boots
programme.desc: Join Maddie, Danny, the Junkyard Helpers and Northern Ballet for CBeebies Puss in Boots

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scooter Boy gets his confidence knocked after a small accident, so Sarah and Duck help him to get it back

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The stubborn little sister asks for a pair of ice skates, but her dad worries she will not want them for long

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo needs to go to the shops, but Tee is more interested in building a rocket out of cardboard boxes

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: In space, Tiny and Small catch the memory box Mother made when she was a little girl. Animated fun, narrated by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka visits the garden to wash everyone's faces, including the tiny Pontipines

programme.title: Hushabye Lullabye
programme.desc: Dillie Dally plays in the winter snow and gets some help building a friendly snow alien.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Joe Wicks tells the tale of a gorilla who really isn't keen on bananas.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of a six-bedroom house full of period features in Newport, south Wales, a two-bedroom flat near Blackpool's Golden Mile with lots of potential and a mid-terrace house in Bradford

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A woman's personal injury claim bites back after claiming that metal shards were found in her takeaway and a bishop faces judgement after trying to pull off a £12,000 car fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers documenting a wreck in the Thames find more than they bargained for and call in the Royal Navy bomb team. An unsafe load flies onto the windscreen of another driver

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams in Brackley, Northamptonshire, and presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out more about German porcelain

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The medics eagerly await Ruhma's return, while Al tries to process his life-changing decision. Valerie has her eyes opened when she receives an invitation to an unusual wedding

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Norfolk, where he catches up with a couple who have developed a new business and a duo now enjoying the simple life in the East Anglian countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving two metal chairs to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a loom chair and cabinet to furniture restyler Chloe Kempster. For his own project, Jay transforms a pair of old wardrobe doors

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the fourth leg of their trip, Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp scour Lancashire for antiques to sell for a profit at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions are frayed for Phil - but will he and the others get away with the heist? Kim encourages Denise to pursue her relationship with Jack as there are clearly still feelings there. Homeless Tina ends up on Shirley's doorstep and makes a desperate plea to her sister

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Scunthorpe woman Caroline Blanchard, a single mum who has twice had to make the decision to turn off the life support for people she loves. She and her children live in the shadow of the garage, where seven-year-old Natasha banged her head in 2007, and the conservatory, where husband and father Paul had a heart attack four years later. The volunteers plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish inspired by seaside, using a choice of fish, shellfish and vegetables at their disposal. But as always, Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing expect to see refined food with an imaginative twist. And it's not over yet, as they then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Tom Parker Bowles, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, the judges will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A woman claiming she tripped on a loose paving stone as she pushed her toddler in a buggy, is caught out when CCTV footage reveals the truth. Plus, information embedded in a digital photo scuppers an attempted scam for lost jewellery. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A skier suffers a leg injury in a remote part of the Alps, so her guide tries to carry her to safety on his back. A girl calls 999 when her mum goes into anaphylactic shock

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion digs up some of the best kitchen and garden antiques that the red and blue teams have purchased around the country over the years. She also shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring antique fairs and centres. Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a unique collection of teapots in Kent, while Eric Knowles delves into the history and collectability of bone china

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protein shake made from dried and powdered crickets, washable cleaning cloths, and an app for locating children that have wandered off

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with two sets of buyers whose rural aspirations were driven by a spirit of adventure. She first heads to the Welsh Borders for a reunion with a couple who managed to buy their first home - a rural farmhouse with space to accommodate their many hobbies and dogs - in 2017. Nicki then moves on to Devon's south coast to revisit a couple of surfing enthusiasts who, after reassessing their priorities, have successfully built a new life by the sea with their young son

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore uses her salvaging expertise at Witley Recycling Centre to search for three items that she can clean up and reinvent. To help her, she calls on trusty makers Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Duncan McKean. Leigh-Anne takes on two wingback armchairs in need of a makeover, while lighting expert Duncan has some retro lampshades to contend with. Sarah herself sees potential in an extendable dining table

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp hunt for antiques in the Derbyshire Dales. They both shop until they drop, buying everything from delicate glassware to hearty rustic pieces, with an animal-shaped cocktail shaker thrown into the mix. Along the way, Margie explores a world of splendour in an historic 550-acre garden, tucked away in a very surprising location. Natasha discovers a renowned artist whose intricate and breath-taking paintings and drawings illuminated the spirit of the age in which he lived

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon warns Linda about her suspicions about Max, but she dismisses it, claiming he is just a friend. But when he turns up later, Linda becomes guarded, wondering if her friend has a point. Ian arranges an interview with the Walford Gazette to talk about his business empire - unaware of what is being revealed behind his back

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games. Host Richard has some surprises up his sleeve, including a House of Games House Band, a sneaky peak at some other rooms in his House of Games and a few surprise, special guests. A weekly winner will be declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show as the scores are tallied across the five-part series, resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz, inviting writer Joan Bakewelll and comedian Fin Taylor to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: In 15 years of playing, Rhod may possibly be the only rugby union player on earth never to score a point. How will he fare when given the role of coaching a team? After a day at the Welsh Rugby Union's National Centre of Excellence, he is put in charge of Whitland RFC's under-8s and expected to prepare the youngsters for a major tournament

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Romesh Ranganathan promotes his new book As Good As It Gets: Life Lessons from a Reluctant Adult and Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens embark on their first Drag Race UK tour. Hosted by Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards, the event sees The Vivienne, Baga Chipz, Divina de Campo, Cheryl Hole, Blu Hydranga, Sum Ting Wong, Vinegar Strokes, Scaredy Kat, and Gothy Kendoll perform in venues in six UK cities. Alyssa - who promises to bring a seasoned and steady hand to the stage - arrives to find the gaggle of UK queens scrambling to get their collective act together. The problem is that curtain-up is only hours away

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: After only three days of rehearsals, the UK queens - along with Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards - raise the curtain on the sold-out opening night of the first-ever UK Drag Race tour. However, a microphone malfunction threatens to silence the show before it even starts

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ollie Dabbous and Asma Khan, and special guest Omid Djalili. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Liverpool v Leicester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about Derby's connection to the hot dog

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. There was an upset in last year's corresponding contest when Stefanos Tsitsipas overcame six-time champion Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite the stars of sci-fi and fantasy to take part in a special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Newcastle and Chelsea were both looking to emulate impressive victories in last season's corresponding encounters

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v Arizona Cardinals

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone

programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to the Isle of Rum where the community is looking for new people to come and live there, having constructed eco-homes for new families. Euan McIlwraith follows in the footsteps of some of the islanders from St Kilda who were evacuated exactly 90 years ago and made a new life on the mainland. Anne Lundon investigates Roan island's unique medical experiments, while over on Luing off the Argyll coast Arlene Stuart finds out about the famous cattle breed developed on the island

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: It's Sunday in the house of the couch-potato clan and Nana calls round. Denise tries on her wedding dress, but nothing can stand in the way of Antiques Roadshow. Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, following the lives of a working-class Manchester family and set almost exclusively in their living room. With Ricky Tomlinson, Sue Johnston and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Romesh Ranganathan promotes his new book As Good As It Gets: Life Lessons from a Reluctant Adult and Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens embark on their first Drag Race UK tour. Hosted by Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards, the event sees The Vivienne, Baga Chipz, Divina de Campo, Cheryl Hole, Blu Hydranga, Sum Ting Wong, Vinegar Strokes, Scaredy Kat, and Gothy Kendoll perform in venues in six UK cities. Alyssa - who promises to bring a seasoned and steady hand to the stage - arrives to find the gaggle of UK queens scrambling to get their collective act together. The problem is that curtain-up is only hours away

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: After only three days of rehearsals, the UK queens - along with Drag Race legend Alyssa Edwards - raise the curtain on the sold-out opening night of the first-ever UK Drag Race tour. However, a microphone malfunction threatens to silence the show before it even starts

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ollie Dabbous and Asma Khan, and special guest Omid Djalili. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Liverpool v Leicester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about Derby's connection to the hot dog

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. There was an upset in last year's corresponding contest when Stefanos Tsitsipas overcame six-time champion Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite the stars of sci-fi and fantasy to take part in a special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Newcastle and Chelsea were both looking to emulate impressive victories in last season's corresponding encounters

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Hibernian v Celtic at Easter Road and Dundee United v Hamilton Academical at Tannadice

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v Arizona Cardinals

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of a six-bedroom house full of period features in Newport, south Wales, a two-bedroom flat near Blackpool's Golden Mile with lots of potential and a mid-terrace house in Bradford

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A woman's personal injury claim bites back after claiming that metal shards were found in her takeaway and a bishop faces judgement after trying to pull off a £12,000 car fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers documenting a wreck in the Thames find more than they bargained for and call in the Royal Navy bomb team. An unsafe load flies onto the windscreen of another driver

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams in Brackley, Northamptonshire, and presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out more about German porcelain

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The medics eagerly await Ruhma's return, while Al tries to process his life-changing decision. Valerie has her eyes opened when she receives an invitation to an unusual wedding

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Norfolk, where he catches up with a couple who have developed a new business and a duo now enjoying the simple life in the East Anglian countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving two metal chairs to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a loom chair and cabinet to furniture restyler Chloe Kempster. For his own project, Jay transforms a pair of old wardrobe doors

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the fourth leg of their trip, Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp scour Lancashire for antiques to sell for a profit at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions are frayed for Phil - but will he and the others get away with the heist? Kim encourages Denise to pursue her relationship with Jack as there are clearly still feelings there. Homeless Tina ends up on Shirley's doorstep and makes a desperate plea to her sister

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Scunthorpe woman Caroline Blanchard, a single mum who has twice had to make the decision to turn off the life support for people she loves. She and her children live in the shadow of the garage, where seven-year-old Natasha banged her head in 2007, and the conservatory, where husband and father Paul had a heart attack four years later. The volunteers plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish inspired by seaside, using a choice of fish, shellfish and vegetables at their disposal. But as always, Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing expect to see refined food with an imaginative twist. And it's not over yet, as they then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Tom Parker Bowles, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, the judges will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a house in London's Plaistow, a shell of a property in Lancashire, and a flat in Stoke-on-Trent as they are sold at auction

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A woman claiming she tripped on a loose paving stone as she pushed her toddler in a buggy, is caught out when CCTV footage reveals the truth. Plus, information embedded in a digital photo scuppers an attempted scam for lost jewellery. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A skier suffers a leg injury in a remote part of the Alps, so her guide tries to carry her to safety on his back. A girl calls 999 when her mum goes into anaphylactic shock

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion digs up some of the best kitchen and garden antiques that the red and blue teams have purchased around the country over the years. She also shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring antique fairs and centres. Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a unique collection of teapots in Kent, while Eric Knowles delves into the history and collectability of bone china

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protein shake made from dried and powdered crickets, washable cleaning cloths, and an app for locating children that have wandered off

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with two sets of buyers whose rural aspirations were driven by a spirit of adventure. She first heads to the Welsh Borders for a reunion with a couple who managed to buy their first home - a rural farmhouse with space to accommodate their many hobbies and dogs - in 2017. Nicki then moves on to Devon's south coast to revisit a couple of surfing enthusiasts who, after reassessing their priorities, have successfully built a new life by the sea with their young son

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore uses her salvaging expertise at Witley Recycling Centre to search for three items that she can clean up and reinvent. To help her, she calls on trusty makers Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Duncan McKean. Leigh-Anne takes on two wingback armchairs in need of a makeover, while lighting expert Duncan has some retro lampshades to contend with. Sarah herself sees potential in an extendable dining table

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp hunt for antiques in the Derbyshire Dales. They both shop until they drop, buying everything from delicate glassware to hearty rustic pieces, with an animal-shaped cocktail shaker thrown into the mix. Along the way, Margie explores a world of splendour in an historic 550-acre garden, tucked away in a very surprising location. Natasha discovers a renowned artist whose intricate and breath-taking paintings and drawings illuminated the spirit of the age in which he lived

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon warns Linda about her suspicions about Max, but she dismisses it, claiming he is just a friend. But when he turns up later, Linda becomes guarded, wondering if her friend has a point. Ian arranges an interview with the Walford Gazette to talk about his business empire - unaware of what is being revealed behind his back

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games. Host Richard has some surprises up his sleeve, including a House of Games House Band, a sneaky peak at some other rooms in his House of Games and a few surprise, special guests. A weekly winner will be declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show as the scores are tallied across the five-part series, resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Writer Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz, inviting writer Joan Bakewelll and comedian Fin Taylor to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: It's Sunday in the house of the couch-potato clan and Nana calls round. Denise tries on her wedding dress, but nothing can stand in the way of Antiques Roadshow. Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, following the lives of a working-class Manchester family and set almost exclusively in their living room. With Ricky Tomlinson, Sue Johnston and Liz Smith

programme.desc: Stewie and Brian's friendship is tested when Brian chews one of Stewie's teddy bear Rupert. Peter meets his favourite cereal mascot Boo Berry at the grocery store and refuses to wash his right hand after shaking it with him

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian posts an offensive tweet that goes viral and the Griffins tell him he has to move out after the residents of Quahog begin to shun them

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers an alternative ending in the Russian version of one of his favourite films and writes to Vladimir Putin, who comes to Quahog. Meanwhile, a strapped-for-cash Brian gets a job at a suicide hotline centre and attempts to strike up a romance with a co-worker

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Jeff and Hayley announce they would like to have a baby, and Stan takes the prospect of becoming a grandfather badly. Meanwhile, Steve plans to become a responsible uncle

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley experiences the hardships of pregnancy when Roger agrees to 'give birth' to Jeff to make him human again. Meanwhile, Snot is offended when Steve signs him up for a makeover

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland tries to keep his vasectomy secret from Donna when she says she wants another baby, but his wife gets her own back on him when she discovers he has been lying to her. Meanwhile, Roberta is annoyed when Rallo wins the lead role in a community theatre production of Annie

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland's birthday present to Freight Train is upstaged by a lavish gift from Robert, but the two men realise they must try to get along when they suddenly become siblings. Meanwhile, Rallo and Cleveland Jr try to raise money to pay off a debt by reuniting lost dogs with their owners

programme.title: The Emily Atack Show
programme.desc: Emily shares her honest musings on family life, and sheds some light on her own childhood with her parents sitting in the audience. Plus, impressions of stars including Stacey Solomon, Holly Willoughby and Keith Lemon

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates another selection of records, including the heaviest train pulled with a beard, the furthest distance as a human arrow and the smallest man's suit

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken is astounded to be told Sue has resigned and left the school. Liz meets with Fraser and agrees to give him an alibi for the night of the warehouse raid

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe warns Paddy to stop spending time up at Outhwaite's farm. Kelly confides in Mandy that she might be pregnant. Kim tells Steve her version of events about the video

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tries to convince Kelly to tell Biff about the baby. Biff wants to make his peace with the Glovers. Zoe watches the video of Kim and Daniels

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. A handsome man from Denmark arrives at the household in search of work, setting Mariette's heart racing with his dashing good looks, but Charley is not impressed by the visitor's charms. Drama, starring David Jason, Catherine Zeta-Jones and Philip Franks

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Louisa struggles to adapt to Larry moving out. The thought of all her children leaving her drives her to set aside her doubts about pursuing a relationship with Hugh, which leads to a revealing conversation about Vasilia. The younger Durrell children are not sure when their mother's birthday is - so plan a party anyway

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy begins a frantic search for David's dog Deefer after a pair of youths steal her car from outside the Aidensfield Arms with the hound in the boot. Carol and Rob have cause for concern as Rosie is troubled by the attentions of an admirer who simply will not take no for an answer - and also has a history of violence against a former girlfriend. Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor and Vanessa Hehir star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: An angry farmer arrives at the Dingles claiming Priscilla the pig is his. Butch continues to antagonise Marlon. Zoe shows Chris the video. Kelly has decided to get an abortion

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tries to talk Kelly out of getting an abortion. Chris wants to know what Zoe intends to do with the video. Sarah apologises to Kathy for falling out with her over Rachel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud is annoyed to find Maureen has taken Sally on full time at the shop. Fiona gets fed up with Alan never returning her calls. Liz is interviewed by the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fraser tells Liz he has sold The Hourglass. Vera is adamant Brad is their grandson when Jack insists he is not. Tricia is surprised when Ray visits the Rovers

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor has his second GMC assessment - mock surgery - and Buddy is missing. Meanwhile, Ken's arrival does not bring good news for Al and Bert, and Penhale unhappy with the arrival of his predecessor, Mark Mylow, who tries to take charge of his investigation. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A relaxed Will is installed as the new vicar, though is only just back at the vicarage when Geordie drops by requesting his help. Initially reluctant to be drawn in, Will is swayed when he learns that the matter concerns Adam, a profoundly deaf boy from a devout family, whose mother has recently died following a fall. Adam's father thinks his son was responsible and the truth needs uncovering to help the family heal. Will's own troubled past threatens to colour his view of the case - and Geordie realises his new friend has demons of his own. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.desc: Text-based information service

Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor and Vanessa Hehir star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike and Sally plan a holiday, but things run far from smoothly when Sid gets involved with the financial planning. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean informs Sid she wants a lover to bring a bit of spice back into her life. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland and Sid James

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mariette and Charley locate Pieter and beg him to stay, while Pop faces hostility from the villagers. Primrose does her best to make an impression on the Reverend Candy. David Jason and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry moves back into the family's villa having come down with mumps, which Louisa hopes will give Aunt Hermione an incentive to cancel her planned visit. Her hopes prove in vain when her overbearing relative turns up, accompanied by medium Mrs Haddock (Una Stubbs), who claims to be able to contact Louisa's husband. She tells Louisa that he is terribly unhappy about her numerous male admirers, fuelling her doubts over her relationship with Hugh, and proposes a seance on a stormy night to set his troubled spirit at rest

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker and Miller struggle to decide whether an ex-Army major is guilty of murder after shooting a youth who broke into his home, while Carol tries to get through to a pregnant teenager. Rosie learns the garage is in financial trouble - unaware Peggy and a salesman with big ideas (impressionist Jon Culshaw) have already dreamed up a plan that could save it. Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara and Kim go shopping for a wedding dress. Chris and Zoe go to Leeds to find Daniels, who has a shock revelation for them regarding the lady in the lake's death

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris cannot believe Zoe has taken Daniels at his word. Tara asks Steve why he is hiding his family from Kim. Andy and Robert set up Jack and Sarah for a night out

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Angie turns up to visit Curly and catch up with all the gossip. Tricia swears to Vera that Brad is Terry's son. Fiona is upset to hear Alan has kept her away from work parties

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin finds Chris sleeping rough in the garage. Don blames Mike for Josie leaving him and marrying someone else. Angie encourages Des to sell up and go off on his boat

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Dr Alan Delaney is killed by a hit-and-run driver while in charge of his partner's new car, Barnaby and Jones discover the dead man had once been accused by Dr James Kirkwood of stealing a large sum of money from their Midsomer Market practice. Further investigations to uncover the whereabouts of the missing money lead the detectives to believe that Kirkwood may have been the real target. Guest starring Stephanie Cole (Doc Martin), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The acerbic GP investigates after local farmer Dennis Thorpe shoots at Robert for taking some of his eggs. Meanwhile, Emily returns to the surgery to see Martin after her acne medication caused a bad reaction, a secret bidder buys the Crab and Lobster pub, and Louisa ponders if she should see a fertility specialist

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will is settling into the new routines of his life in Grantchester when an unexpected phone call reveals his father has got himself into some trouble, so he asks Geordie for his help. Will hasn't been home in a while, running from a past he wants to forget, but he cannot outrun his roots for ever and when one vicious beating leads to a grisly murder at a sprawling ancestral home, scandal threatens to engulf the Davenport family - suggesting life may never be the same for Will again. Jemma Redgrave and Nathaniel Parker guest star, alongside Robson Green and Tom Brittney

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is delighted when Mike announces his intention to marry into wealth - until a close link between the two families is uncovered

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Carry On Henry (1971)
programme.desc: King Henry VIII plots to rid himself of his new French wife when her craving for garlic becomes too much to cope with. However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

Will hasn't been home in a while, running from a past he wants to forget, but he cannot outrun his roots for ever and when one vicious beating leads to a grisly murder at a sprawling ancestral home, scandal threatens to engulf the Davenport family - suggesting life may never be the same for Will again. Jemma Redgrave and Nathaniel Parker guest star, alongside Robson Green and Tom Brittney

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is delighted when Mike announces his intention to marry into wealth - until a close link between the two families is uncovered

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Carry On Henry (1971)
programme.desc: King Henry VIII plots to rid himself of his new French wife when her craving for garlic becomes too much to cope with. However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Norman's overbearing mother tries her hand at matchmaking, while Figgis's visitors cause him some anxiety. Comedy, with James Bolam, Christopher Strauli and Peter Bowles

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar poses as a jailbird in an attempt to learn who is responsible for organising a series of audacious mass prison escapes. Vintage drama, guest starring Donald Sutherland, with Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The men send for Marion when Robert is badly wounded in a raid. Meanwhile, the Sheriff of Nottingham is eager to prove that Robert is the outlaws' leader - already being referred to by the people as Robin Hood

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: In order to investigate a villain's idea of the perfect crime, Harriet poses as a prostitute and Jim goes up in the world. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry tries to help his friend Kate evict some unwanted tenants, but comes to doubt her motives. Meanwhile, Arthur is desperate to get into the property market and tries to sell Terry's flat without telling him. Guest starring Toyah Willcox and Simon Cadell

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is joined by an old friend - Turkish policeman Captain Shebbeq - to help him track down a gold-smuggling ring. As he observes a secret government deal to transport the precious commodity, he comes to suspect there is something rather sinister also within the cargo. Nadim Sawalha guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar saves the life of the prime minister of a British colony. Guest starring Edward Woodward

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: A girl asks Robin to help safeguard an ancient treasure cared for by her father, who has foreseen his own death and wishes to appoint a successor while there is still time. Cathryn Harrison and Cyril Cusack guest star

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The pair are caught between two rival gangs in a world of extortion, murder and kidnapping. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry takes a job driving a Mercedes, but it gets stolen while his back is turned, and to make matters worse he is accused of stealing a cache of diamonds concealed in the car. Vintage comedy, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An expert sniper with a high-powered imported rifle holds a family hostage in a high-rise flat, planning to target a royal visitor at the Wimbledon tennis tournament. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is assigned to ensure the safe shipment of a gold bullion consignment, threatened by the plans of a brilliant computer expert bent on revenge. Drama, guest starring George Cole and John Hurt, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from Glasgow answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: On the Road
programme.desc: Featuring a test of the ultimate open-top sports cars, Porsche's new electric car, and a taste of movie magic with the Mustang Bullitt. Plus, a trip to the French Riviera to try out the all-new Bentley Flying Spur

programme.title: The Bourne Identity (2002)
programme.desc: A fishing boat crew plucks a man out of the sea with two bullet wounds in his back and a safety-deposit number sewn into his skin - but absolutely no memory of who he is or how he got there. In the search for his true identity, he is pursued by assassins - but his skill at evading them suggests his own past is far from straightforward. Thriller, starring Matt Damon, Franka Potente, Chris Cooper, Clive Owen and Brian Cox

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: King John strips the Sheriff of his power for failing to capture the outlaws, and replaces him with Philip Mark, a man renowned for his ruthlessness. Meanwhile, Nasir encounters a fellow Saracen whose combat skills eclipse his own

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Two spies obtain information about a secret missile base intending to pass it to the other side via a person known as Captain Caruso, whom Steed and Mrs Peel are tasked to locate. Vintage adventure, with Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gives shelter to a friend who has broken out of prison, but his act of charity backfires, costing poor old Arthur dearly. Derek Thompson (Casualty) and Sherrie Hewson (Loose Women) feature alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A visiting American senator is assassinated in broad daylight. After a newspaper publishes the names of witnesses, Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect them, but the task soon gets out of hand. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis. A team of five from Holyhead take on the Tenable Tower as they attempt to win a £125,000 prize. Can they score a Perfect 10 to bring home the money?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Coronation Street actress Kym Marsh (Michelle Connor), relationship expert Tracey Cox, choreographer Louie Spence and former tennis star Greg Rusedski compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Quantum of Solace (2008)
programme.desc: James Bond sets out on a personal mission of vengeance as he pursues the secret criminal organisation Quantum. His search leads him to the head of an ecological corporation, who is plotting to orchestrate a coup in a Latin American country to get his hands on a precious natural resource. Spy thriller sequel, starring Daniel Craig, Olga Kurylenko, Judi Dench, Gemma Arterton and Mathieu Amalric

Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two former cellmates attempt to throw a suspicious Regan off their trail by pretending that they have fallen out and that one has murdered the other. Maureen Lipman guests stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Minder

Brian Glover guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.desc: Rishi Persad and the team look ahead to today's racing from Haydock Park and Ascot

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from final meeting of the season from Brands Hatch. With commentary by David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports from Louise Goodman

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin introduces some of the great racehorses of the modern era, including Nijinsky, Tiger Roll, Best Mate, Desert Orchid and Shergar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In the heart of Central America lurks a marine creature believed to reach 8ft in length and weigh up to 300lbs. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is taken to breaking point as he re-evaluates everything he knows about fishing in an attempt to catch this monster - which is known to leap into boats and cause fatal injuries

programme.title: Guns of the Magnificent Seven (1969)
programme.desc: Surviving gunslinger Chris is hired by Mexican revolutionaries to rescue their leader from a military prison. After assembling a crack team including a knife-fighting expert and a firearms aficionado, the group embarks on its mission - but underestimates the depths to which its enemies will sink. Second sequel to the Western, starring George Kennedy, James Whitmore, Monte Markham, Joe Don Baker and Reni Santoni

programme.desc: Cheers owner Sam Malone turns up in Seattle to ask for advice on whether to go through with his wedding - but Frasier recognises his old friend's fiancee as a woman he slept with three months previously. Comedy, guest starring Ted Danson and Tea Leoni

programme.desc: The shrink casually wanders into Daphne's bedroom, making her furious with him. He apologises, but inadvertently does it again and she threatens to leave, forcing him to shower her with gifts to make her stay. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Simpsons

Meanwhile, Principal Skinner falls for a new music teacher. Guest starring Kristen Wiig (Bridesmaids) and Alyson Hannigan (How I Met Your Mother)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes obsessed with a father-son relationship in a 1980s sitcom, using it as a source of inspiration to deal with Bart's hankering for a must-have mini-bike. programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Channel 4 News

Danni Menzies has five options for their £150,000 budget, but whether the couple will be impressed enough to put in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns

She also visits an archaeological dig on Burrow Island and a lantern show at the New Theatre Royal, and discovers the brutal punishments doled out for even minor offences by the Victorian penal system

However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. programme.desc: The isles of Lewis and Harris form Britain's largest offshore island, with a rich history dating back thousands of years. Susan seeks out the home of the world-famous Harris Tweed, visiting a local weaver where she tries her hand at making some of the fabric. Then in Habost, she learns how to Step Dance - a centuries old practice that was almost lost from Scotland for ever. At Callanish, Susan visits some awe-inspiring Neolithic giants - 49 standing stones, predating Stonehenge and steeped in mystery. Nearby, she learns the secret to jewellery making that captures the texture of these ancient standing stones

programme.desc: A foreign correspondent working in occupied Norway is assigned to an RAF detail helping freedom fighters to sabotage an important German U-boat installation. Second World War adventure, with Hugh Williams, Deborah Kerr and Ralph Richardson

Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio

programme.desc: When a CEO threatens drastic action to improve the financial performance of the Chicago branch of her company, the affected manager - who is also her brother - throws an epic festive party to impress a client and close a potential sale which would save jobs. Comedy, starring Jason Bateman and Jennifer Aniston

programme.desc: When a young woman is abducted by a couple from a suburb of Perth, Australia, her only chance of surviving the terrible ordeal her kidnappers inflict upon her is to drive a wedge between them. Crime thriller, starring Emma Booth, Ashleigh Cummings and Stephen Curry

programme.title: Random Acts
programme.desc: Black film-maker Denise Alder brings West African art alive in Mablo Micasso's Dream, a surreal and beautiful journey through culture and myth. This is showing as part of Channel 4's programming for Black History Month, which offers an important platform for different aspects of the Black experience in modern Britain
